outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: UAE to quit OPEC in blow to world's leading oil exporters, as Iran war roils energy sector
outlets.summary: CNN leads with UAE's shock exit from OPEC effective May 1, stripping the cartel of its third-largest producer. Frames as war-driven economic realignment. Also covers Hezbollah drone killing Israeli contractor in Aitaroun and Houthi record-range attack.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Deadlock over Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz cripples peace efforts
outlets.summary: NPR frames the deadlock analytically — nuclear preconditions blocking any Hormuz deal. UAE OPEC exit and World Bank energy surge warning featured prominently. War Powers Act deadline two days away.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump bashes Iran peace proposal as Tehran looks to Russia for backing; UAE quits OPEC
outlets.summary: Fox News continues with Trump's 'state of collapse' framing while adding UAE OPEC exit coverage. Rubio's 'better than expected' but still rejected Iran proposal featured. Haredi riots condemned by Netanyahu. Hezbollah drone kill in Aitaroun.

outlets.name: Washington Times
outlets.headline: Trump slams Germany's Merz over his Iran comments as transatlantic rift widens
outlets.summary: Right-leaning outlets frame the Trump-Merz clash as European weakness on Iran. Merz's criticism of US strategy seen as undermining Western unity. Townhall covers Treasury's 35-entity sanctions crackdown.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war: What's happening on day 60 as diplomacy gathers pace?
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera leads with comprehensive Day 60-61 overview: UAE quits OPEC, Iranian army declares 'still in war situation,' GCC summit demands Hormuz reopening, Guterres UNSC appeal. War Powers deadline and Hezbollah drone attacks featured. Frames ceasefire as increasingly fragile.

outlets.name: Reuters/BBC
outlets.headline: Gulf leaders meet in Saudi Arabia for first time since start of war on Iran
outlets.summary: International wires lead with GCC Jeddah summit demanding Hormuz reopening. UAE OPEC exit dominates as structural shift. France24 covers Putin hosting Araghchi. CNBC amplifies Merz's 'humiliated' criticism.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Haredi anti-draft rioters break into Military Police chief's home with family inside; Hezbollah drone kills contractor in S. Lebanon
outlets.summary: Times of Israel leads with dual crises: ultra-Orthodox rioters storming Military Police chief's home (25 arrested, Netanyahu condemns) alongside Hezbollah drone killing Israeli contractor Amer Hujairat in Aitaroun. 2 IDF soldiers wounded by separate drone. Ceasefire unraveling.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Civilian contractor working for Defense Ministry killed in southern Lebanon
outlets.summary: Haaretz emphasizes the unprecedented Israel-Gulf military cooperation. Analysis frames this as new era of Middle East security architecture. Concern about provoking further Iranian escalation against Gulf states.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran claims over dozen US Reaper drones worth $330M destroyed; army declares 'wartime conditions'
outlets.summary: Press TV leads with claims of major US drone losses and the Iranian army spokesman declaring 'conditions remain wartime' despite ceasefire. Araghchi's Putin meeting and 'strategic partnership' framing prominent. Steel export ban framed as resilience under siege.

outlets.name: Tehran Times
outlets.headline: Iran's Araghchi: 'Great friends and allies like Russia' — strategic partnership deepens amid war
outlets.summary: Iranian media highlights Araghchi-Putin meeting as diplomatic victory. Putin's praise of Iranian 'courage' featured prominently. Steel export ban framed as temporary measure pending reconstruction. UAE OPEC exit covered as vindication of Iran's stance against Gulf cooperation with US.
